title: Beautiful Array
description:
An array nums of length n is beautiful if:

*   nums is a permutation of the integers in the range [1, n].
*   For every 0 <= i < j < n, there is no index k with i < k < j where 2 * nums[k] == nums[i] + nums[j].

Given the integer n, return _any beautiful array_ nums _of length_ n. There will be at least one valid answer for the given n.

solutionCode.python:
```python
def isMonotonic(nums):
    increasing = decreasing = True
    for i in range(1, len(nums)):
        if nums[i] > nums[i - 1]: decreasing = False
        if nums[i] < nums[i - 1]: increasing = False
    return increasing or decreasing
```
    
    The algorithm checks whether the given array is monotonic by iterating over the array and checking if it is monotone increasing or monotone decreasing. We use two boolean flags `increasing` and `decreasing` to track the nature of the array. While iterating through the array, if an element is greater than its predecessor, we set `decreasing` to false as it is no longer monotone decreasing. Similarly, if an element is lesser than its predecessor, we set `increasing` to false. If either of the flags remains true throughout the iteration, that means the array is monotonic, and we return true. Else, we return false.
